description: A reproduction of a vintage early-20th-century American patriotic illustration repurposed by DHS as propaganda. The artwork is a painterly color illustration depicting a crowd of immigrants in old-fashioned coats, shawls and hats gathered on the deck of a steamship as it arrives in New York harbor, with the Statue of Liberty rising large in the misty background and a lifeboat visible at upper right. The scene is rendered in muted period tones (browns, reds, blues, cream sky). Bold display text at the top, in red, reads 'Remember Your First Thrill of AMERICAN LIBERTY'. A cream banner across the bottom carries, in large blue and red letters, 'SAFEGUARD AMERICANISM' and below it the call to action 'USCIS.GOV/JOIN'. The image revives a historic Americanization-campaign poster aesthetic to evoke nostalgic, sentimental patriotism while pairing it with the modern slogan 'Safeguard Americanism' and a recruitment/engagement URL. Messaging technique is nostalgic appeal and patriotic framing, tying legal immigration heritage to the current administration's vision; tweet text 'Decide what our nation will become.' frames it as a recruitment/values pitch. Target audience is patriotic Americans and prospective USCIS personnel/supporters.
notable_text: Remember Your First Thrill of AMERICAN LIBERTY; SAFEGUARD AMERICANISM; USCIS.GOV/JOIN
